concerns.text: Task T032 requires ingesting a 'baseline reaction time dataset (simulated or real)'. The spec.md (Assumptions) and plan.md explicitly state the dataset does not contain individual baseline speed data. Introducing 'simulated' data violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle I (Reproducibility) as it creates a non-verified, non-checksummed input that contradicts the defined 'Merged Dataset' entity.

Task T035 mandates a sensitivity analysis for 'arousal' using a 'proxy variable'. The spec.md (Assumptions) and Edge Cases explicitly define arousal/micro-climate as unmeasured noise or a limitation to be reported, not a variable with an available proxy. This task demands implementation of a feature that the spec declares impossible with the available data, effectively weakening the constraint to 'report limitation' into 'find a proxy'.

Task T012 specifies fetching NOAA data from 'Numenta NAB or UCI'. The plan.md and spec.md (FR-001) strictly define the canonical sources as 'NOAA GHCN-Daily or ERA5-Land'. Using Numenta NAB (anomaly detection benchmark) or generic UCI datasets violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and the specific data provenance constraints of the plan.

Phase 6 (T032-T035) introduces a 'Baseline Reaction Time Correction' workflow that is not present in the spec.md or plan.md. The spec assumes no baseline data exists. This task list silently relaxes the constraint that the analysis must rely solely on the Moral Machine dataset, introducing a 'staged' simplification (simulation) without explicit spec authorization, violating the 'No silent constitution drift' rule.
